metadata.disclaimer: Educational information only. Not legal advice.

eligibility.title: 🗳️ Voter Eligibility
eligibility.summary: Who can vote in Indian elections

rules.residency: Must be an ordinary resident of the constituency
disqualifications: Unsound mind (as declared by competent court)
Disqualified under any law related to corrupt practices

registration.title: 📋 Voter Registration
registration.summary: How to register as a voter in India

steps: Visit voters.eci.gov.in or download the Voter Helpline App
Select 'New Voter Registration' and fill Form 6
Upload passport photo and supporting documents
Submit and note the reference number
Track status online using reference number
Booth Level Officer (BLO) may visit for verification
Once approved, name appears in electoral roll and EPIC is issued
Obtain Form 6 from ERO/BLO office or download from eci.gov.in
Fill in personal details, address, and constituency
Attach self-attested copies of required documents
Submit to the Electoral Registration Officer (ERO)

forms.Form 7: Objection to inclusion / request for name deletion
forms.Form 8: Correction of entries / shifting of residence / replacement of EPIC

requiredDocuments: Proof of Identity (Aadhaar, Passport, PAN, Driving License)
Proof of Age (Birth Certificate, School Leaving Certificate, Aadhaar)
Proof of Address (Aadhaar, Utility Bill, Bank Statement, Rent Agreement)

election_timeline.title: 📅 Election Timeline
election_timeline.summary: The 9 phases of an Indian election

phases.name: Schedule Announcement
phases.description: ECI announces election dates. Model Code of Conduct takes immediate effect.

phases.name: Issue of Notification
phases.description: Formal notification issued to commence electoral process.

phases.name: Nomination Filing
phases.description: Candidates file nomination papers with the Returning Officer.

phases.name: Scrutiny of Nominations
phases.description: Returning Officer examines nomination papers for validity.

phases.name: Campaigning
phases.description: Parties and candidates campaign publicly. Must end 48 hours before polling.

phases.name: Silence Period
phases.description: No campaigning allowed. 48 hours before polling.

phases.name: Polling Day
phases.description: Voters cast ballots at designated polling stations.

phases.name: Counting & Results
phases.description: Votes counted under supervision. Results declared same day.

polling_booth.title: 🏛️ Polling Day Process
polling_booth.summary: Step-by-step: what happens when you vote

steps.name: Arrive & Queue
steps.description: Go to your designated polling station. Join the queue.

steps.name: Identity Verification
steps.description: Present your EPIC (Voter ID) or authorized photo ID to the First Polling Officer.

steps.name: Register & Ink
steps.description: Second Polling Officer verifies details, applies indelible ink to left index finger, takes signature/thumb impression on Form 17A.

steps.name: Machine Authorization
steps.description: Third Polling Officer checks ink mark and activates the EVM ballot unit for you.

steps.name: Cast Vote
steps.description: Enter the voting compartment. Press the blue button next to your chosen candidate on the Ballot Unit.

steps.name: VVPAT Verification
steps.description: VVPAT screen displays candidate name, symbol, and serial number for ~7 seconds. A beep confirms the vote is recorded.

steps.name: Exit
steps.description: Leave the polling station. Your democratic duty is complete! 🎉

acceptableIDs: Student ID (if issued by recognized institution)

voter_rights.title: ⚖️ Voter Rights
voter_rights.summary: Your democratic rights as an Indian voter
rights: Right to vote by secret ballot
Right to know criminal record, assets, and education of candidates (via ECI affidavits)
Right to use NOTA (None of the Above) to reject all candidates
Right to a voter slip showing booth details
Right to assistance if physically disabled (companion allowed)
Right to file complaints about electoral malpractice
Right to a free and fair election environment
grievanceMechanisms: cVIGIL App for real-time reporting of violations

faq.question: What is NOTA?
faq.answer: NOTA (None of the Above) is an option on the EVM ballot unit that allows a voter to officially reject all contesting candidates. It was introduced by the Supreme Court in 2013.

faq.question: What is ECI?
faq.answer: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering Union and State election processes. It was established on January 25, 1950.

faq.question: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
faq.answer: The MCC is a set of guidelines issued by the ECI for political parties and candidates during elections. It comes into force from the date the election schedule is announced and remains in effect until results are declared.

faq.question: What is an EVM?
faq.answer: An Electronic Voting Machine is a portable device used for casting and counting votes. It consists of a Control Unit and a Ballot Unit, and is paired with a VVPAT for vote verification.

faq.question: What is VVPAT?
faq.answer: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail is a device attached to EVMs that prints a paper slip showing the candidate's name and symbol, allowing the voter to verify their vote before it is stored.

faq.question: What is a Booth Level Officer?
faq.answer: A BLO is an official appointed by the ERO for each polling booth area. Their role is to update the electoral roll, verify new registrations, and assist voters in their jurisdiction.

faq.question: What is EPIC?
faq.answer: Electors Photo Identity Card (EPIC), commonly known as Voter ID Card, is the primary identity document issued to registered voters by the Election Commission of India.
